name: esbuild
what_it_does: Extremely fast JavaScript and TypeScript bundler and minifier written in Go, offering 10–100x speed improvements over webpack or Rollup for bundling and transpilation tasks.
use_cases: Bundle a TypeScript library for npm publication with tree-shaking and minification in a single CLI call
Transpile and bundle a large JavaScript codebase for multiple browser targets (esm, cjs, iife) in parallel
Use the Node.js API (@esbuild/core) to integrate bundling into a custom build pipeline or agent workflow
Serve a development build with esbuild's built-in HTTP server and watch mode for fast iteration
Process JSX/TSX files and emit browser-ready bundles without a heavy webpack configuration
not_for: TypeScript type-checking — esbuild strips types but does not run tsc; run tsc separately for type safety
Complex plugin ecosystems requiring webpack loaders — esbuild's plugin API is simpler and not compatible with webpack/Rollup plugins
CSS-heavy projects needing PostCSS transforms — esbuild has basic CSS support but lacks a PostCSS plugin pipeline
best_when: You need maximum bundling speed for CI pipelines or large codebases and can accept a simpler plugin API compared to webpack.
avoid_when: Your project relies on TypeScript type checking at build time or requires webpack-compatible loader plugins.

auth.notes: Local build tool — no authentication needed.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Error messages are concise but sometimes lack line-level context for transform errors; resolve errors include file paths.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Builds are deterministic given the same inputs and esbuild version.
known_agent_gotchas: esbuild does NOT perform TypeScript type checking — agents must invoke tsc --noEmit separately to catch type errors
Plugin API is not compatible with Rollup or webpack plugins; agents must find esbuild-specific plugins or write custom ones
The 'external' option must explicitly list all Node.js built-ins (e.g., 'fs', 'path') when targeting Node or they will be bundled
Output format 'esm' does not add file extensions to imports by default; downstream consumers may need explicit extensions for Node ESM
Watch mode runs indefinitely — agents invoking esbuild programmatically with watch:true must manage process lifecycle explicitly

security_score_components.security_notes: No network calls; runs entirely locally. Dependency count is low (primarily Go stdlib). Agents should ensure they pin esbuild versions to avoid supply-chain drift.
